concept.conceptName: Assessment using Numeric Pain Rating Scale

en.name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NPRS, also Numeric Rating Scale or NRS) is an 11-point (0–10) self-reported scale on which the patient rates the intensity of their pain, where 0 corresponds to "no pain" and 10 to the "worst pain imaginable" [1]. The scale is widely used in clinical practice because of its simplicity, brevity, and applicability to a wide range of populations including patients unable to use a continuous visual analogue scale. The original validation study compared the NRS with a simple descriptive scale and the visual analogue scale, and demonstrated that the 11-point NRS performs better than both alternatives [1]. ## Included Concepts This concept set contains the SNOMED concepts representing a recorded NPRS score and the procedure of assessing it: - `37151630` — Numeric Pain Rating Scale score (`1284857008`, Observable Entity) - `37174733` — Assessment using Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284856004`, Procedure) ## Excluded Concepts The following SNOMED concept is intentionally **not** included, as it represents the assessment tool rather than the recorded score value: - `37151627` — Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284852002`, Staging / Scales) ## References [1] Downie WW, Leatham PA, Rhind VM, Wright V, Branco JA, Anderson JA. Studies with pain rating scales. *Ann Rheum Dis*. 1978;37(4):378-381. doi:10.1136/ard.37.4.378. PMID: 686873

fr.name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
fr.longDescription: ## Définition et contexte clinique La NPRS (Numeric Pain Rating Scale, ou échelle numérique NRS) est une échelle d'auto-évaluation à 11 points (0–10) sur laquelle le patient cote l'intensité de sa douleur, où 0 correspond à « aucune douleur » et 10 à la « pire douleur imaginable » [1]. Elle est largement utilisée en pratique clinique du fait de sa simplicité, de sa brièveté et de son applicabilité à un large éventail de populations, y compris les patients ne pouvant pas utiliser une échelle visuelle analogique continue. L'étude princeps de validation a comparé la NRS à une échelle descriptive simple et à l'échelle visuelle analogique, et a montré que la NRS à 11 points est plus performante que ces deux alternatives [1]. ## Concepts inclus Ce concept set contient les concepts SNOMED représentant un score NPRS enregistré ainsi que la procédure d'évaluation : - `37151630` — Numeric Pain Rating Scale score (`1284857008`, Observable Entity) - `37174733` — Assessment using Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284856004`, Procedure) ## Concepts exclus Le concept SNOMED suivant est intentionnellement **non inclus**, car il représente l'outil d'évaluation et non la valeur du score enregistré : - `37151627` — Numeric Pain Rating Scale (`1284852002`, Staging / Scales) ## Références [1] Downie WW, Leatham PA, Rhind VM, Wright V, Branco JA, Anderson JA. Studies with pain rating scales. *Ann Rheum Dis*. 1978;37(4):378-381. doi:10.1136/ard.37.4.378. PMID: 686873

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France
createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.summary: Removed SNOMED Staging/Scales concept 37151627 (Numeric Pain Rating Scale, Standard). Rationale: this concept set is intended to identify recorded pain scores; the remaining Observable Entity (37151630, Numeric Pain Rating Scale score) and assessment Procedure (37174733) are the relevant concepts.
